patch.value: Nightly Validation (ubuntu-latest, Node 24) on ed7cd5b6 failed with 'Coverage for branches (99.99%) does not meet global threshold (100%)' while ALL tests passed. Sole uncovered branch: src/core/store/front-matter-cache.ts:505 — `if (!existing || shouldReplaceCachedDocumentCandidate(...))`. Whether `existing` is ever truthy during the cache scan depends on directory enumeration order (which duplicate-id document is read first), so the short-circuit's second arm is only exercised when readdir returns entries in a particular order — Node 24 on ubuntu enumerates differently and the branch goes dark. Prior nightlies on Node 24 passed (3605c829, 02b91bac), confirming flake rather than regression; the daily release gate is unaffected (product tests green). Fix direction: add a deterministic unit spec that seeds the cache state with an existing candidate for the same id and asserts both replace and keep outcomes of shouldReplaceCachedDocumentCandidate through this call site, removing the readdir-order dependency from coverage. Run: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/actions/runs/28922530777 Source: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/482
GH-482: Node 24 nightly coverage gate flake — readdir-order-dependent branch at front-matter-cache.ts:505

A deterministic spec drives both arms of the existing/replace branch at front-matter-cache.ts:505 regardless of directory enumeration order; nightly Node 24 coverage gate passes repeatedly; GH-482 closed with the run evidence

author: codex-root-20260709
message: Bundle deterministic Node 24 cache-coverage regression with the broad SDK context relevance PR

value.author: codex-root-20260709
value.text: Implementation start: live GH-482 remains open even though the 2026-07-09 nightly recovered. This bundle will remove readdir-order dependence by exercising both duplicate-id replacement decisions deterministically, then attach 100% coverage and current nightly evidence.

value.note: Make duplicate candidate selection and complexity deterministic across filesystem order

value.author: codex-root-20260709
value.text: Evidence update: extracted the pure shouldRecordCachedDocumentCandidate decision used at the scan call site, with deterministic unseen-id, replace, and keep assertions independent of readdir or Promise completion order. Changed-file CodeFactor parity exposed readCachedDocumentParts complexity 17; collection selection was extracted and the file now passes the <=16 gate. Focused 26-test cache/release contract suite, typecheck, ESLint, and static quality all pass.

value.author: codex-root-20260709
value.text: Final local evidence: deterministic unseen/replace/keep candidate decision is independent of directory enumeration; current 2026-07-09 Node 24 Nightly Validation run 29002197585 passed; full local suite passed 5,501 tests with exact 100/100/100/100 coverage. GH-482 will be closed after merged-head CI evidence.

value.author: codex-root-20260709
value.text: PR #487 cache review follow-up: both cache-hit and cache-miss heavy-metadata selection now share selectHeavyMetadata. Rejected the split?.heavy warning with code-path evidence: !needRead returns before parsing, while every fallthrough path initializes parsed and split before access. Full coverage remains exact 100%.
